firstWeek.label: Confirm your move-in date and where official mail can reach you
firstWeek.description: Clarify where you can receive important letters (municipality, IND, bank) and what your next housing step is. Registration and your BSN depend on having a valid address where you can receive post; confirm with your landlord or host that mail in your name is accepted.

firstWeek.label: Prepare documents for municipality registration
firstWeek.description: Have the documents you need ready before your registration appointment: passport, proof of address (e.g. rental contract), and any required civil or residence documents. Check your municipality’s website for the exact list so your appointment is not postponed.

firstWeek.label: Receive your BSN and note which steps depend on it
firstWeek.description: After registration, note your BSN and list which next steps need it: bank account, DigiD, health insurance, and employer. Sequencing these avoids delays and ensures you meet deadlines (e.g. health insurance within four months).

firstWeek.label: Open a Dutch bank account
firstWeek.description: Open an account once you have BSN and proof of address; employers and landlords typically expect it, and direct debits for rent and insurance are easier with a local account.

firstWeek.label: Set up mobile connectivity for daily admin
firstWeek.description: A Dutch mobile number helps you receive OTPs and confirmations from banks, DigiD, and other services.

firstWeek.label: Track which tasks must wait for a stable address
firstWeek.description: Know which tasks you can do now and which must wait until you have a stable address; plan how you will get there.

firstWeek.label: Align household admin ownership
firstWeek.description: When moving together, agree who handles registration, banking, and insurance so nothing is missed.

firstWeek.label: Review school or childcare follow-up if needed
firstWeek.description: List and schedule school or childcare registration and related admin.

firstMonth.label: Arrange Dutch health insurance
firstMonth.description: Take out basic health insurance (basisverzekering) once you are resident. It is mandatory within four months of registration; late enrolment can mean backdated premiums and penalties. You will need your BSN and usually a bank account for the premium.

firstMonth.label: Apply for DigiD when eligible
firstMonth.description: Apply once you have BSN and address; you will receive an activation code by post, so apply early.

firstMonth.label: Register with a GP (huisarts)
firstMonth.description: Contact local practices early; availability and waiting times differ by area.

firstMonth.label: Set up recurring payments
firstMonth.description: Organise direct debits for rent, insurance and utilities so nothing is missed.

firstMonth.label: Set up your local transport routine
firstMonth.description: Work out how you will get to work, appointments and daily errands.

firstMonth.label: Track admin follow-ups weekly
firstMonth.description: Review pending tasks once a week so confirmations and next actions do not get lost.

firstMonth.label: Confirm your next housing decision date
firstMonth.description: If your housing is temporary, set a date by which you will secure longer-term housing.

firstMonth.label: Review childcare, school and family registration follow-up
firstMonth.description: School and childcare registration, plus any family-specific admin, often need follow-up in the first month.

firstMonth.label: Confirm salary payment account readiness
firstMonth.description: Ensure your employer has the right account details and first payment is on track.

firstMonth.label: Set up a support routine for practical local questions
firstMonth.description: Reliable contacts and saved notes make recurring admin easier.
